items.q: What is the difference between annulment and divorce in Catholic teaching?
items.correct: An annulment declares a sacramental marriage was never valid in the first place; divorce claims to end a valid marriage, which the Catholic Church does not recognise.
items.misconception: Annulment is simply the Catholic word for divorce.
items.random_wrong: An annulment is a blessing given on a wedding anniversary.
items.why: Students treat the two as identical, but the Catholic position only allows annulment precisely because it holds a valid sacramental marriage cannot be dissolved.

items.correct: No — divorce is permitted in Islam as a last resort after attempts at reconciliation, followed by a waiting period (iddah), though hadith describes it as disliked by Allah.
items.misconception: Divorce is absolutely forbidden for Muslims in all circumstances.
items.random_wrong: Muslim divorce requires the approval of a Christian court.
items.why: Students assume all religions ban divorce like traditional Catholic teaching, missing Islam's distinctive position of permitted-but-discouraged.

items.q: Do all Christians hold the same view on contraception?
items.correct: No — official Catholic teaching opposes artificial contraception (Humanae Vitae, 1968), while most Protestant churches accept it as a responsible choice within marriage.
items.misconception: All Christians are forbidden from using any form of contraception.
items.random_wrong: Contraception is mentioned in the Ten Commandments.
items.why: Students generalise the Catholic position to all Christianity; exam answers must contrast denominational views to reach higher levels.

items.correct: A couple living together without being married
items.why: It's A couple living together without being married. “A trial separation before divorce” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What is the Islamic marriage contract called?
items.why: Correct answer: Nikah. “Mahr” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which 1968 papal encyclical prohibited artificial contraception for Catholics?
items.why: It's Humanae Vitae. “Rerum Novarum” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: A sexual relationship with someone other than one's spouse
items.why: It's A sexual relationship with someone other than one's spouse. “Marrying more than one person” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What is the maximum number of wives a Muslim man may marry under Islamic law?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 wives. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
